body.title: Fix: ticket IDs corrupted to [REDACTED-CREDENTIAL] by CC privacy filter
body.result: Added [REDACTED guard in cmd_add; recreated T-google-secretary-dispatcher with clean ID; cancelled corrupted rows
body.intention: I intend that ticket IDs are never silently corrupted by CC's privacy filter.
body.description:
Two tickets had their IDs corrupted to the literal string '[REDACTED-CREDENTIAL]' in clan.memories. Root cause: CC's privacy filter intercepted the cc_queue.py output during ticket creation and replaced values it classified as credentials. The corrupted rows were deleted 2026-06-05; one GoogleSecretary Dispatcher ticket needs to be recreated with ID T-google-secretary-dispatcher-refactor.

Fix: either (1) make cc_queue.py write the DB directly without going through CC Bash output, OR (2) add a validation step that rejects IDs matching [REDACTED*]. The corrupted rows were both gated on T-google-secretary-device anyway so no work was lost.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py
**Design rules:** queue is black box; all writes via cc_queue.py
**Scope boundary:** In scope: guard against corrupted IDs at write time; recreate the one deleted ticket. Out of scope: retroactive history fix.
**Completion criteria:** cc_queue.py add raises a clear error when id field matches '[REDACTED' pattern; GoogleSecretary Dispatcher ticket recreated with proper ID.
